title: Read a count then that many u32 integers and write their sum, or error on any failure
prompt: Read line 1 as N, a decimal integer count in the range 0 to 1000 inclusive. Then read N more lines, each a decimal unsigned integer that fits in u32 (range 0 to 4294967295 inclusive). Sum all N values and write the sum as decimal followed by a single newline. If any of the following hold, write `error\n` instead: line 1 fails to parse as u32 or exceeds 1000; fewer than N additional value lines are available; any value line fails to parse as u32; or the running sum overflows u32 (would exceed 4294967295). Trailing whitespace on each line is tolerated and trimmed before parsing. Leading zeros are accepted; leading `+`/`-` signs are not. The empty sum (N=0) is `0\n`. Do not write to standard error. Exit with status 0 in every case.

input_schema.stdin: line 1: N (u32 in 0..=1000). lines 2..N+1: u32 values.

output_schema.stdout: either `<decimal_sum>\n` on success or `error\n` on any failure

notes_input_convention: Zero reads N from argv[1] and the N values from argv[2..N+1] (no exposed stdin in Zero 0.1.2). The values are interpreted identically to the (N+1) stdin lines other languages read. The verifier passes a per-case argv list to the Zero binary and the same values on stdin (newline-separated) to non-Zero refs. This task is the second slot in the error-handling arc: it exercises a counted loop where ANY mid-iteration failure (parse error or running-sum overflow) collapses to the same `error\n` output.
